_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 Alaska State Senators (Stevens, Rauscher, Kaufman, Bjorkman, Kiehl). State tier. Evidence-first: every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. AK Senate has a bipartisan majority coalition, so caucus != issue position — scored from actual votes/sponsorships/ratings/self-statements.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: retiring, NOT running in 2026 (announced May 2025); prior flag 'incumbent_seeking_reelection' was stale. Scored on current voting record (Freedom Index) only; 1996 VoteSmart NPAT social positions are 30 years stale and intentionally NOT scored.

gary-stevens.notes_set: Long-serving Republican Senate President (Kodiak, SD-C) who leads the bipartisan Senate Majority coalition (9 D + 8 R). RETIRING — announced May 2025, not running in 2026; term runs through Jan 2027 (Louise Stutes announced for the open seat). Current voting record is low on the conservative index: John Birch Society Freedom Index lifetime 13% (0% current session) — votes for expanded spending, against limiting the Governor's COVID emergency powers, and led 2025 Senate overrides of GOP Gov. Dunleavy's education-funding vetoes. Voted NO on defunding Critical Race Theory. A 1996 VoteSmart NPAT showed conservative positions on abortion exceptions, opposing same-sex marriage (pre-Obergefell), school vouchers, and easing gun restrictions, but these are 30 years stale and not scored as current positions. No current evidence located on most life/marriage/gun/election/immigration cells; left null.

2.note: Freedom Index records him voting NO on 'Defunding Critical Race Theory' — i.e., he opposed defunding CRT.
Freedom Index (lifetime 13%) documents consistent votes for expanded government spending and against property-tax protections; led 2025 overrides of the Governor's education-funding vetoes.

profile.religion: Christian (active missionary work; co-chairs the Alaska Legislative Prayer Caucus and Legislative Prison Ministry)
profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06. Appointed to the AK Senate (SD-O) Nov 2025 from the House; in the Republican minority; filed to run for the seat in 2026. Scored from prime/co-sponsorships, official bio, and candidate-site statements.

george-rauscher.notes_set: Republican State Senator (SD-O, Mat-Su), appointed Nov 2025 after moving up from the House (2017-2025); sits in the Republican minority. Strongly conservative documented record. Sanctity of life: prime sponsor of SB 271 (34th), a fetal-heartbeat abortion ban with civil enforcement; states 'life begins at conception and abortion is the killing of a living human being.' Christian liberty: co-chairs the Alaska Legislative Prayer Caucus and Legislative Prison Ministry; longtime missionary. Self-defense: NRA lifetime member; prime sponsor HJR 3 (concealed-carry reciprocity), cosponsor HB 61 (limitations on firearm restrictions) and HB 143 (firearm financial privacy). Public justice: cosponsor HB 286 (crime victim restitution) and HB 159/SB 207 (tougher property crimes); prime sponsor HB 108 (peace-officer retirement). Federal overreach: prime sponsor HB 83 (Citizens' Advisory Commission on Federal Areas), HJR 2 (reverse offshore oil-gas ban), HJR 26 (state jurisdiction over coastal waters). Family/child: cosponsor HB 254 (block porn to minors), HB 265 (child sexual abuse material), HJR 28 (Kids Online Safety Act). Also prime sponsor of HB 1/HB 19 to repeal ranked-choice voting/open primaries, and cosponsor of specie (gold/silver) legal-tender bills (economic q1 is N/A at state tier). No documented marriage/sexuality, immigration, or CBDC/balanced-budget positions located.

0.note: Prime sponsor of SB 271 (fetal-heartbeat abortion ban); states 'life begins at conception and abortion is the killing of a living human being.'

3.note: Cosponsor HB 254 (block pornography to minors / social media age-verification), HB 265 (child sexual abuse material), HJR 28 (Kids Online Safety Act).

0.note: Longtime missionary (Haiti, Indonesia, Russia; with Franklin Graham); publicly professes Christ — co-chairs Legislative Prayer Caucus.

3.note: Co-chairs the Alaska Legislative Prayer Caucus (prayer in a public body).

0.note: Prime sponsor HJR 3 (concealed-carry reciprocity); NRA lifetime member; supports broad carry/2A rights (AK has permitless carry).

1.note: Cosponsor HB 61 (limitations on firearm restrictions) and HB 143 (firearm financial privacy — blocks gun-purchase merchant-category tracking).

4.note: Cosponsor HB 143 firearm financial privacy — opposes surveillance/tracking of gun purchases.

0.note: Prime sponsor HB 108 (peace-officer retirement benefits); co-chairs Legislative Prison Ministry — backs law enforcement.

2.note: Prime sponsor HB 83 reviving the Citizens' Advisory Commission on Federal Areas — resists federal land overreach.

3.note: Prime sponsor HJR 2 (urge reversal of federal offshore oil/gas ban); cosponsor HJR 26 (state jurisdiction over coastal water) — affirms state sovereignty.

4.note: HB 83 (federal-areas commission) and HJR 26 (coastal water jurisdiction) oppose federal capture of state land/waters.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06. Holds SD-F (South Anchorage); won a 4-year term in 2024, NOT up until 2028. Was in the bipartisan Senate majority 2023-24, then moved to the GOP minority Dec 2024. The disputed 2024 AK Family Council survey (which he disowned as 'fake news,' conceded an error by the council) is NOT used; scored from his 2020 AKFC endorsement, sponsored fiscal legislation, and official record.

james-kaufman.notes_set: Republican State Senator (SD-F, South Anchorage); won a 4-year term in 2024 (next up 2028). Joined the Democrat-inclusive bipartisan Senate majority 2023-24, then moved to the GOP minority Dec 2024. Signature work is fiscal: prime author of SJR 4 / SB 20 (a GDP-based constitutional appropriation/spending cap) and budget-process reform (SB 21, SB 25, HB 187); sits on Senate Finance. 2020 AK Family Council endorsement: pro-life self-statement ('not right to kill anyone if you can avoid it, particularly the unborn'), supports defunding Planned Parenthood, favors school choice, opposes SOGI laws / supports traditional Christian views on marriage. Public safety: fallen-peace-officer plates, backed VPSO expansion (SB 81), longtime community-patrol volunteer. Federal overreach: prime sponsor SB 34 (Citizens' Advisory Commission on Federal Areas). On ranked-choice voting he was comfortable with the mechanics (advised against the GOP 'Rank Red' strategy) — not a repeal advocate. The 2024 AK Family Council survey attributed to him was disowned by Kaufman and conceded as an error by the council — not used here. No documented gun, immigration, or CBDC positions located.

0.note: 2020 AK Family Council endorsement: pro-life self-statement ('it is not right to kill anyone if you can possibly avoid it, particularly the unborn'); supports defunding Planned Parenthood.
2020 AK Family Council endorsement characterizes him as supporting traditional Christian views on marriage and sexuality.
AK Family Council (2020): 'favors school choice'; Senate site supports expanded educational options.

2.note: Prime author of SJR 4 / SB 20 — a GDP-based constitutional appropriation (spending) cap; opposes new taxes absent a spending cap.

0.note: Sponsored fallen-peace-officer specialty plates; backed VPSO expansion (SB 81); longtime Coalition of Community Patrols / HALO volunteer.

2.note: Prime sponsor SB 34 reestablishing the Citizens' Advisory Commission on Federal Areas — resists federal land-management overreach.

4.note: SB 34 (federal-areas commission) opposes federal capture of state land.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06. Holds SD-D (Kenai); won a 4-year term in 2024, NOT up until 2028. Member of the bipartisan Senate majority; former teacher and KPEA (teachers' union) board member. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: self-describes pro-life but Alaska Right to Life scores his life votes adversely.

jesse-bjorkman.notes_set: Republican State Senator (SD-D, Kenai Peninsula); won a 4-year term in 2024 (next up 2028). Member of the bipartisan Senate majority coalition; former Nikiski teacher and KPEA (teachers' union) executive-board member 2014-2019; commercial fisherman. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION ON LIFE: self-describes as pro-life, but Alaska Right to Life's 'Life Review 33' documents him OPPOSING the Life at Conception Act (HB 205) and NOT supporting the abortion-prohibition bill (HB 215), voting for contraceptive-coverage and Planned-Parenthood-aligned measures, and notes he joined the Democrat-led coalition — scored FALSE on life per the documented votes. Parental rights: voted NO on SB 240 (which would let minors 16+ consent to behavioral/psychiatric services without parental consent) — pro-parental-consent. Pro-2A: NRA member, Safari Club board, backed the Kenai Borough 2nd Amendment Sanctuary ordinance. Public safety priority ('support Troopers, keep criminals in jail'). Federalism: voted YES on HJR 10 (opposing federal control of state wildlife management). Education: voted to override the Governor's school-funding vetoes (SB 140, HB 69) with the coalition; supports homeschool families. JBS Freedom Index lifetime 28%. On RCV he said he'd vote for the 2024 repeal 'with reservations.'

0.note: Alaska Right to Life 'Life Review 33' lists him as having OPPOSED the Life at Conception Act (HB 205) — documented contrary to personhood-from-conception.

1.note: AK Right to Life: 'Not Supported' on the abortion-prohibition bill (HB 215); voted for contraceptive-coverage and PP-aligned measures.

0.note: Candidate statement priority: 'Support Homeschool Families and Community Schools'; op-ed notes increasing homeschool/correspondence funding.

1.note: Voted NO on SB 240, which would let minors 16+ consent to behavioral/psychiatric services without parental consent — pro-parental-consent.
Self-identified NRA member; as a Kenai Borough Assembly member backed the 2020 2nd Amendment Sanctuary ordinance 'because of threats to the Second Amendment.'

0.note: Candidate-statement priority: 'Prioritize Public Safety — Support Troopers & keep criminals in jail.'

2.note: Voted YES on HJR 10 (federalism / opposing federal control over state wildlife and hunting management).

4.note: HJR 10 opposes federal preemption of state land/wildlife management.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06. Holds SD-B (Juneau); won a 4-year term in 2024, NOT up until 2028. Democrat in the bipartisan majority; Senate Finance. Searched genuinely for any conservative-aligned position to credit — none documented; scored FALSE only where there is a documented contrary vote/statement, null elsewhere.

jesse-kiehl.notes_set: Democratic State Senator (SD-B, Juneau); in office since 2019; won a 4-year term in 2024 (next up 2028); sits on Senate Finance. Consistently progressive documented record with no conservative deviation found. Life: opposed SJR 4 (abortion constitutional amendment), urged voters to reject the 2022 constitutional-convention question, endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates (2024). Sexuality: the lone Senate Judiciary member to vote against the transgender-girls'-sports ban (2022), opposed restricting transgender students' rights, and as Juneau deputy mayor led an LGBTQ-inclusive non-discrimination ordinance. Guns: one of only 3 Senate votes against HB 61 (gun-store-protection bill), calling 2A a 'special rights' issue, and supports a red-flag law. Economic: argues Alaska needs new taxes/revenue; backed a ~$1K PFD. No conservative credit earnable from the available record; cells without documented contrary evidence left null (not penalized).

0.note: Opposed SJR 4 (abortion amendment), urged rejecting the 2022 constitutional convention sought to enable abortion limits, endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates — documented pro-abortion-rights, contrary to personhood-from-conception.

1.note: Pro-abortion-rights record; does not advocate abortion abolition.

2.note: Lone Senate Judiciary member to vote against the bill banning transgender girls from female school sports (2022) — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Opposed restricting transgender students' rights; as Juneau deputy mayor led an LGBTQ-inclusive non-discrimination ordinance — supports LGBTQ promotion in policy.

1.note: One of 3 Senate votes against HB 61 (gun-store protection), called the Second Amendment a 'special rights' issue, and supports a red-flag law.
